note: Methods are learnable practices distinct from abilities — techniques anyone can attempt (skill-gated by check, not by power-system), and the tools that enable them. The Rune Dice are the flagship: a divination method that reads the pole-intensities of a place, person, or moment — tying divination directly to the coordinate-world model. Methods resolve through the normal engine (a check + GM narration); tools grant/enable them. Authored by Aevi (PO).

methods.name: Rune-Casting
methods.description: Casting the rune dice to read the hidden shape of a place, a person, or a moment — which forces are present and how hot. A cast does not predict the future; it reads the PRESENT truly, surfacing the pole-intensities the caster couldn't otherwise perceive.
methods.howItWorks: The caster throws the dice and reads the faces that land. Each face maps to a pole; the faces that come up, and how they fall together, name which forces are lit in the thing cast-upon and roughly how strongly. Mechanically: a successful cast reveals a target's poleIntensity to the player (the fog lifts on that node's true nature); a strong success also reveals a co-firing the caster wouldn't have guessed (a jewel surfacing). A failed cast reads noise; a botch reads a plausible LIE — the dice can be wrong, and a caster who trusts a bad throw walks into it.
methods.gmGuidance: Use rune-casting to reveal a location's or NPC's pole-intensities when the player casts — it's the in-world instrument for the coordinate model. At a doubly-charged jewel, a good cast should name the improbable overlap ('death here, in all this life'). Never let it read the future; it reads what IS, harder to see.
methods.learnedFrom: Heights rune-readers, Wend road-casters, or self-taught with a set and time.
methods.notFor: Prophecy or fortune-telling (it reads the present, not the future); reading a mind's contents (only its pole-disposition); certainty (a botch lies).

methods.name: Coordinate-Reading
methods.description: Reading the axis-gradients of the land to know which way the world leans and how far to the next Reach — navigating the coordinate-world as geography.
methods.howItWorks: A successful read reveals adjacent nodes' rough direction and purity (which way is 'toward order,' how far to a pole, where a March lies). Ties travel to the graph: the reader sees edges before walking them.
methods.gmGuidance: Lets a player scout the graph a step ahead — reveal dim/heard-of nodes and their dominant pole without committing to the journey.
methods.notFor: Seeing a fogged pole's interior; safe passage guarantees (knowing the way is not surviving it).

methods.name: Attunement
methods.description: A quiet practice of matching one's own pole-disposition to a place or moment — sensing, and briefly holding, its true character. The folk-root of Numenwork's True Attention.
methods.howItWorks: On success, the practitioner reads the felt truth of the current scene (its hot poles) without a tool, and steadies themselves or another against its pull. Repeated attunement to one pole nudges the practitioner's own drift toward it — attention shapes the attender.
methods.gmGuidance: A tool-free, always-available way to surface a scene's character for a patient player; also the mechanism by which a character slowly becomes what they attend to.
methods.learnedFrom: Anyone who sits still long enough; formally, the Numinous.
methods.notFor: Commanding a place; fast results (attunement takes real time).

tools.name: The Rune Dice
tools.description: A set of hand-cast dice — traditionally six, of dark stone, each face marked with a pole-glyph. No two makers' sets read quite alike, and a caster's own set, carried long enough, starts to fall truer for them and stranger for anyone else. Enables Rune-Casting; a fine set eases the read.
tools.flavor: The dice do not decide. They show. What you do with the showing is yours, and so is the mistake if the throw was a liar.

evolution.note: A set carried and cast often attunes to its caster — reading truer for them, and refusing to read cleanly for a thief. Tool evolves on use alone.

stages.name: A Maker's Set
stages.description: Good stone, honest faces, no history with you yet.
stages.narrationHints: They land the way any dice land — truly enough, if you can read them.

stages.name: Your Set
stages.description: The dice have learned your hand — they fall truer for you, and read muddy for anyone who lifts them.
stages.grant: Rune-Casts ease; a stolen set reads noise for the thief.
stages.narrationHints: They warm to your throw now, and cool to a stranger's.

stages.name: The Speaking Stones
stages.description: Cast long enough, the dice begin to answer questions you didn't ask aloud — and once in a while, before you throw.
stages.grant: Once per scene the dice may surface a reading unprompted — a warning, a hidden pole, a co-firing about to matter; GM-offered, at the edge of the useful and the eerie.
stages.narrationHints: Sometimes they land before you've decided to throw. You've stopped being sure who's asking.

tools.name: Axis-Compass
tools.description: A Lattice-made instrument of nested rings that swing toward whichever pole runs strongest nearby — crude at a distance, precise up close. Enables Coordinate-Reading; the Wend prefer their own bones-and-memory method and call the compass a crutch.

tools.name: Reader's Cloth
tools.description: A marked casting-cloth that gives the rune dice a field to fall on and read against — the marks help sort a muddy throw. Rolls up small; every serious caster keeps one.
